name: intermittent-unclear
description: 3 traces with 'connection timeout' on retrieval over 6 hours. 4% error rate vs 2% baseline. Not conclusive.

item.title: Intermittent retrieval timeouts on customer-support-bot
item.description: Automated alert: connection timeout errors detected on customer-support-bot retrieval step.
item.note:
Investigated the retrieval timeout reports. Findings:

- 3 traces over a 6-hour window (08:00-14:00 UTC) had "connection timeout" errors on the vector DB retrieval span
- Trace IDs: int-2201 (08:22 UTC), int-2287 (10:41 UTC), int-2350 (13:55 UTC)
- No obvious pattern in timing — roughly every 2-3 hours but irregular
- Total traces in the window: 74. Error rate: 4.05% (vs 2% baseline)
- The 4% is above baseline but based on only 3 occurrences — not statistically significant
- The retrieval span normally takes 120-300ms; failed spans timed out at 5000ms
- No errors on email-classifier or any other agent
- Vector DB dashboard shows no reported outages, but I can't see internal connection pool metrics
- All 3 failed traces had different user sessions and query types — no content similarity
- The agent did NOT recover in any of the 3 cases — traces returned an error to the user

Inconclusive. Could be transient network issues or early sign of vector DB instability. Need more data to determine pattern.

projectMemory:
## Project Agents
- **customer-support-bot**: Handles customer inquiries via RAG. Typical trace has 3-5 LLM calls, 1-2 retrieval steps. Normal latency: 2-4s per trace.
- **email-classifier**: Classifies incoming emails. Single LLM call per trace, ~500ms.

## Baselines
- Error rate: <2%
- Daily cost: ~$5
- Trace volume: ~200/day
